[2018-07-02 22:17] zheliu (Zhe): Hi I'm new im reading this and have no idea what these terms are
[2018-07-02 22:17] zheliu (Zhe): What are seeds and inflation
[2018-07-02 22:18] awaclus: seeds mean how many copies of that unit are available
[2018-07-02 22:20] awaclus: inflation means that if you buy a Drone this turn, next turn you have the Drone and the gold it produced (i.e. 4 gold worth of stuff), so the gold this turn is worth more than the gold next turn since your purchases will continue to generate value throughout the game
[2018-07-02 22:22] zheliu (Zhe): I don't get that part
[2018-07-02 22:22] zheliu (Zhe): Why is the gold this turn worth more than next turn
[2018-07-02 22:22] awaclus: because it costs 3 gold now to buy a drone
[2018-07-02 22:23] awaclus: if you do, next turn you will have 4 gold worth of stuff
[2018-07-02 22:23] supergreyman (Greyman): Also, ever wonder when you should stop making Tarsiers and start making Rhinos to attack? Knowing inflation helps here as well.
[2018-07-02 22:24] awaclus: that's why the default inflation is 33%, but with some units it can be higher if they produce a lot of value really fast and sometimes it can be lower if building drones isn't what you want to do
[2018-07-02 22:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): inflation is the rate of which you want stuff now vs stuff later
[2018-07-02 22:25] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, that's like the most general definition.
[2018-07-02 22:26] zheliu (Zhe): But a drone isn't really worth 4 gold the next turn right it generates 1 per turn and only has 1 health unless you value 1 health as 3 gold
[2018-07-02 22:26] zheliu (Zhe): So did it really increase from 3 to 4 gold worth
[2018-07-02 22:27] awaclus: yes because the drone costs 3 gold
[2018-07-02 22:27] awaclus: so having the drone is worth 3 gold
[2018-07-02 22:27] supergreyman (Greyman): It's the gold value you have in the board state.
[2018-07-02 22:28] zheliu (Zhe): How much do you generally value each point of health and attack
[2018-07-02 22:29] supergreyman (Greyman): Easier example, if you put your $100 in your bank with interest rate of 1% monthly, you'll get $101 next month.
[2018-07-02 22:29] awaclus: as a rule of thumb, 1 health is the exact same as 1 point of attack (as in a Pixie, not as in a Tarsier), and that's worth around 2 gold
[2018-07-02 22:29] zheliu (Zhe): Yeah so what I'm thinking is like if you buy a drone this turn that costs 3 gold right but the next turn immediately after you gain 1 gold and the hp is worth around 2 gold so on your board it's still about 3 gold worth even though it's a drone
[2018-07-02 22:30] awaclus: we're assuming that you want the drone anyway
[2018-07-02 22:30] supergreyman (Greyman): The value of a drone in term of health isn't meaningful until you start to actually defend with it.
[2018-07-02 22:30] awaclus: so if you buy it now, it costs 3 gold and generates one gold next turn, but if you buy it next turn, it still costs 3 gold but doesn't generate a gold that turn
[2018-07-02 22:31] zheliu (Zhe): Yeah so if you don't value the hp at all it takes 3 turns for the drone to even break even in cost efficiency
[2018-07-02 22:31] zheliu (Zhe): Like it doesn't generate you any new gold until third turn
[2018-07-02 22:31] f300xen (f0x): i mean you can block with it
[2018-07-02 22:32] zheliu (Zhe): Yeah so to me it doesn't seem like drone is 133% efficiency the turn after it's built
[2018-07-02 22:33] awaclus: but the drone in and of itself is worth 3 gold because that's what it costs to buy one
[2018-07-02 22:33] zheliu (Zhe): Yeah but imagine you have a 10 gold drone unit that does the same thing as a drone and has 1 hp
[2018-07-02 22:34] zheliu (Zhe): You wouldn't say that thing is worth 11 gold the next turn
[2018-07-02 22:34] zheliu (Zhe): It's worth barely anything
[2018-07-02 22:34] namington: thats why 1.32 is the stated figure for drone inflation, not 1.33 😉
[2018-07-02 22:34] awaclus: if I spent 10 gold on that thing for whatever reason, it is clearly worth that much to me
[2018-07-02 22:34] mrguy888: drone is worth less than 3 in the later stages of the game
[2018-07-02 22:35] mrguy888: drones are ❤ if you will
[2018-07-02 22:35] awaclus: yeah it doesn't hold up if you don't actually buy the unit
[2018-07-02 22:35] apooche: Since drone is worth less than three later on, that should mean inflation is actually lower than 1.28 early
[2018-07-02 22:35] apooche: Or drone would be a bad purchase
[2018-07-02 22:35] mrguy888: when drone is the best unit to buy it is worth 3
[2018-07-02 22:36] namington: if i could buy steelsplitter t1 instead of DD
[2018-07-02 22:36] mrguy888: well splitters cost more than 6
[2018-07-02 22:36] mrguy888: if opponent could buy a 5 gold no blue wall turn 1 i wouldnt buy splitter instead of dd
[2018-07-02 22:37] namington: energy costs 0 except for the first few turns tho, with a couple of exceptions
[2018-07-02 22:37] mrguy888: energy costs more than 0 early though which is the turns we are talking about
[2018-07-02 22:37] apooche: Right, I guess that’s why no one buys engineers in the early game
[2018-07-02 22:37] mrguy888: you literally buy engineer for more energy
[2018-07-02 22:37] namington: thats true but my point is
[2018-07-02 22:37] namington: is inflation really a useful method for evaluating cost
[2018-07-02 22:38] awaclus: it doesn't necessarily cost more than 0 if you go for a build that skips drones anyway

[2018-07-02 22:38] namington: like yeah obv inflaiton is useful once youre transitioning into the midgame

[2018-07-02 22:39] awaclus: >I got a great idea. Lets do math and ignore important things that are not convenient and don't support my point.
pulling numbers out of your ass and doing math with them is always better than pulling the answer directly out of your ass
[2018-07-02 22:39] namington: drone is inefficient
[2018-07-02 22:39] mrguy888: due to absorb and wasting tech
[2018-07-02 22:39] namington: relative to other units
[2018-07-02 22:39] apooche: Yeah what are you guys crazy
[2018-07-02 22:39] namington: so before you pass the absorb barrier
[2018-07-02 22:39] namington: your attackers arent doing anything
[2018-07-02 22:39] namington: once you pass the absorb line, attackers quickly become more efficient than drones
[2018-07-02 22:40] namington: and thus drones become worse and worse purchases
[2018-07-02 22:40] zheliu (Zhe): Oh I see so early on attack is worth less than later on
[2018-07-02 22:40] zheliu (Zhe): So drone worth more relatively
[2018-07-02 22:40] namington: it costs just as much, but because youre not over the absorb barrier it's not helping you much, yes.
[2018-07-02 22:40] mrguy888: You have to pay a large flat cost to unlock the superior investment of attackers
[2018-07-02 22:40] namington: obv you still need attack to get over the absorb threshold in the first place though
[2018-07-02 22:40] mrguy888: that flat cost is the absorb barrier
[2018-07-02 22:40] awaclus: well, more specifically, if it's a game with no better defenders than Wall, the first two attack points per turn are worth nothing and each point after that is worth ~2 gold
[2018-07-02 22:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): there's also the fact that defending becomes harder the higher the attack number
[2018-07-02 22:41] namington: "1 damage = 2 gold" is a very rough estimate (and ignores stuff like the half-turn advantage) but yeah
[2018-07-02 22:42] Deleted User (Deleted User): so when defending is hard you want attack and defense (and gold which buys them) now
[2018-07-02 22:42] Deleted User (Deleted User): otherwise, you're happy to have them later
[2018-07-02 22:42] awaclus: >"1 damage = 2 gold" is a very rough estimate (and ignores stuff like the half-turn advantage) but yeah
yeah and also dependent on the situation
[2018-07-02 22:42] namington: dont trust cooledcannon he buys out drone supply before buying any tech building
[2018-07-02 22:42] awaclus: but if you're killing an engineer, that means your opponent has to spend 2 to replace it
[2018-07-02 22:43] zheliu (Zhe): What's half turn advantage
[2018-07-02 22:43] namington: they have to spend that 2 gold preemptively tho
[2018-07-02 22:44] mrguy888: half turn advantage is like how p2 gets an extra drone for being second
[2018-07-02 22:44] namington: @Zhe idk if theres an intuitive way to put it but basically, you have to build defense on your turn to prep for attack on your opponents next turn, so your defense needs to be bought a half-turn before your opponent actually deals the damage
[2018-07-02 22:45] namington: yeah, its related to the inherent advantage p1 gets that requires p2 to need a seventh drone to balance it
[2018-07-02 22:45] mrguy888: so 6 now is worth 7 half a turn later
[2018-07-02 22:45] zheliu (Zhe): Does anyone know win rates between p1 and p2
[2018-07-02 22:45] zheliu (Zhe): Is there a significant difference
[2018-07-02 22:45] mrguy888: prismata-stats.com is the site right?
[2018-07-02 22:45] mrguy888: you can look at stats there
[2018-07-02 22:46] namington: yeah conveniently (7/6)^2 is almost exactly drone inflation
[2018-07-02 22:46] awaclus: overall they're extremely close but there are some sets that are somewhat favored one way or the other
[2018-07-02 22:46] namington: so its fairly balanced numbers-wise
[2018-07-02 22:46] namington: player advantage generally comes from tech being easier for one player to acquire than the other
[2018-07-02 22:46] namington: for example, p2 is favoured in very green or cheap red heavy sets
[2018-07-02 22:46] namington: because of natural conduit or fastimus
[2018-07-02 22:47] mrguy888: yeah the starting positions are fair but the option each player has each turn is radically different
[2018-07-02 22:47] mrguy888: sometimes those different options arent fair

[2018-07-05 22:03] swapgo: Man the more I play Thermite sets the more I hate the unit
[2018-07-05 22:06] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): lol, is absorb denial so bad? it's been part of the same since I started playing, so it's just another strategy.
[2018-07-05 22:07] swapgo: I mean Absorb Denial is fine, it's just that Thermite lets you effectively cut through anything ressembling absorb on the very cheap
[2018-07-05 22:08] swapgo: It's not like Lancetooth or Bloodrager where you piggyback of the opponent build a wall to get something meatier, it's just, constant, blatant disregard of absorb
[2018-07-05 22:08] swapgo: You basically put pressure without building attack
[2018-07-05 22:09] willflame: still, the pressure is a one-time use thing
[2018-07-05 22:09] swapgo: Alright lets put it like this
[2018-07-05 22:10] swapgo: You have a Wall and two Engineer, your opponent has 2 Attack and they bank them into Pixies
[2018-07-05 22:10] swapgo: Essentially, those two engineers might as well be dead, because when they crack the pixies they'll die
[2018-07-05 22:10] swapgo: so you have to keep buying defense for the damage they're not dealing

[2018-07-05 22:11] swapgo: It's easier to manage when there's vigilant stuff, sure, to force them to actually go to the damage step
[2018-07-05 22:12] xujhan: You can also simply not invest much money in absorb. Getting full value out of Thermite often forces you into awkward builds.
[2018-07-05 22:12] cogito3 (Cogito): eh, I definitely wouldn't want every game to be a thermite game, but once in a while it's interesting imo

[2018-07-05 22:21] cogito3 (Cogito): not to mention that once you have 8 attackers, what do you do?
[2018-07-05 22:21] amalloy: you can think of the sum total of your thermite cores as acting a bit like 2 tarsiers, if they're denying your opponent wall absorb. 2 tarsiers for 1B is very good, but if you want to keep scaling attack while also denying absorb, you have to invest another 1B for those same 2 tarsiers. if you buy all 4, you've paid 4BBBB (over several turns) for 2 tarsiers. still a good deal, but it made the rest of your build awkward
[2018-07-05 22:22] cogito3 (Cogito): they're like tarsiers with lifespan though, which is significantly worse
[2018-07-05 22:23] cogito3 (Cogito): I'm actually starting to wonder if more than two thermites is worth it outside of specific situations (valk, other ways to deny absorb, etc)
[2018-07-05 22:23] amalloy: yes, and the fourth thermite has much lower lifespan, often only a turn or two
[2018-07-05 22:23] cogito3 (Cogito): unless there are other ways to deny absorb, yeah
[2018-07-05 22:24] amalloy: the thing is, if you've been denying absorb and scaling attack, inflation is quite high by the time you're thinking of buying the 3rd or 4th thermite. so the lifespan isn't that important
[2018-07-05 22:24] amalloy: it's still good value
[2018-07-05 22:26] cogito3 (Cogito): but if inflation is high, I wonder if it's better just to pop the pixies and get actual attackers
